sources.notes: Statement and constraints: for each query, sum f(prefixes of the queried substring), where f(t) is the maximum split into non-empty prefixes of t.
Used to confirm the per-query KMP prefix-function solution and DP f_i = f_{i-g_i}+1.
Chinese solution used to cross-check that g_i is the shortest border derived through prefix links, and that O(nq) works because q is small.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution index; the article listed in sources was the actually consulted Luogu source.

annotation.summary: For each substring query s[l..r], compute the sum over all prefixes of the maximum number of pieces into which that prefix can be split, where every piece must be a prefix of the whole queried substring.
annotation.constraints: Sum n <= 1e6 and q <= 100, so recomputing linear string data per query is acceptable.
annotation.core_idea: For a string t, let g_i be the length of the shortest border of t[1..i], or i itself if no proper border exists. The optimal last piece in the maximum split has length g_i, so dp[i] = dp[i-g_i] + 1. The shortest border can be derived from the KMP prefix function: the border of a border is also a border, so following/compressing prefix links yields g_i. For every query, run KMP on the queried substring and sum the resulting dp values.
annotation.complexity: O(length of query) per query, O(total n * q) in the worst case allowed by q <= 100.
tricks: maximum split uses the shortest border as the last piece
border of a border is also a border

solution_variants.name: per-query-kmp-border-dp
solution_variants.summary: For each query substring, compute prefix-function and shortest-border lengths, then use dp[i]=dp[i-g_i]+1 and sum dp over prefixes.

tags.evidence: The shortest border lengths for every prefix are computed from the KMP prefix function and compressed prefix links.
The key recurrence depends on the shortest border of each prefix.
The value f for each prefix is computed by the recurrence dp[i]=dp[i-g_i]+1.
The accepted approach recomputes KMP and DP independently for each query because q is small.
